heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Ernst Lubitsch. Written by Lubitsch (uncredited) and Edwin Justus Mayer, based on the story by Melchior Lengyel.
clean: Directed by Ernst Lubitsch. Written by Lubitsch (uncredited) and Edwin Justus Mayer, based on the story by Melchior Lengyel.

heading: Joseph Tura
raw: If I shouldn't come back, I forgive you what happened between you and Sobinski. But if I come back, it's a different matter.
clean: If I shouldn't come back, I forgive you what happened between you and Sobinski. But if I come back, it's a different matter.

heading: Joseph Tura
raw: Well, Colonel, all I can say is... you can't have your cake and shoot it, too.
clean: Well, Colonel, all I can say is... you can't have your cake and shoot it, too.

heading: Maria Tura
raw: [to her husband] You're the greatest actor in the world. Everybody knows that, including you.
clean: You're the greatest actor in the world. Everybody knows that, including you.

heading: Other
raw: Anna: What a husband doesn't know won't hurt his wife.
clean: What a husband doesn't know won't hurt his wife.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: [disguised as Siletsky, speaking in reference to his wife, Maria] Her husband is that great, great Polish actor, Joseph Tura. You've probably heard of him.
clean: Her husband is that great, great Polish actor, Joseph Tura. You've probably heard of him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Colonel Ehrhardt: Oh, yes. As a matter of fact I saw him on the stage when I was in Warsaw once before the war.
clean: Oh, yes. As a matter of fact I saw him on the stage when I was in Warsaw once before the war.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Really?
clean: Really?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Colonel Ehrhardt: What he did to Shakespeare we are doing now to Poland.
clean: What he did to Shakespeare we are doing now to Poland.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maria Tura: It's becoming ridiculous the way you grab attention. Whenever I start to tell a story, you finish it. If I go on a diet, you lose the weight. If I have a cold, you cough. And if we should ever have a baby, I'm not so sure I'd be the mother.
clean: It's becoming ridiculous the way you grab attention. Whenever I start to tell a story, you finish it. If I go on a diet, you lose the weight. If I have a cold, you cough. And if we should ever have a baby, I'm not so sure I'd be the mother.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: I'm satisfied to be the father.
clean: I'm satisfied to be the father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Stage Manager: This is all wrong.
clean: This is all wrong.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Makeup Man: What's wrong with it?
clean: What's wrong with it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Stage Manager: I don't know... it's not convincing. To me, he's just a man with a little mustache.
clean: I don't know... it's not convincing. To me, he's just a man with a little mustache.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Makeup Man: But so is Hitler.
clean: But so is Hitler.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dobosh: [motioning to Siletsky] Now you take him back to the hotel.
clean: Now you take him back to the hotel.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Alright.
clean: Alright.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dobosh: As soon as you're in his room, you hit him over the head with the butt of the gun.
clean: As soon as you're in his room, you hit him over the head with the butt of the gun.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Alright.
clean: Alright.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dobosh: Then you take his keys, open his trunk and burn the papers. And then you shoot him.
clean: Then you take his keys, open his trunk and burn the papers. And then you shoot him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Alright. [He turns to leave, then turns back] Just a minute... what'll happen to me? They'll kill me.
clean: Alright. Just a minute... what'll happen to me? They'll kill me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dobosh: Well... we're going to keep our fingers crossed.
clean: Well... we're going to keep our fingers crossed.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Good. [Once again turns to leave, then turns back] Hey, wait a minute... you go to the hotel and I'll cross my fingers.
clean: Good. Hey, wait a minute... you go to the hotel and I'll cross my fingers.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: It's unbelievable! Unbelievable! I come home to find a man in the same boat with me and my wife says to me, "What does it matter?"
clean: It's unbelievable! Unbelievable! I come home to find a man in the same boat with me and my wife says to me, "What does it matter?"

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sobinski: But, Mr. Tura, it's the 'zero hour'.
clean: But, Mr. Tura, it's the 'zero hour'.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maria Tura: You certainly don't want me to waste a lot of time giving you a long explanation.
clean: You certainly don't want me to waste a lot of time giving you a long explanation.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: No, but I think a husband is entitled to an inkling.
clean: No, but I think a husband is entitled to an inkling.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: [disguised as Colonel Ehrhardt] I can't tell you how delighted we are to have you here.
clean: I can't tell you how delighted we are to have you here.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Siletsky: May I say, my dear Colonel, that it's good to breathe the air of the Gestapo again. You know, you're quite famous in London, Colonel. They call you Concentration Camp Ehrhardt.
clean: May I say, my dear Colonel, that it's good to breathe the air of the Gestapo again. You know, you're quite famous in London, Colonel. They call you Concentration Camp Ehrhardt.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Tura: Ha ha. Yes, yes... we do the concentrating and the Poles do the camping.
clean: Ha ha. Yes, yes... we do the concentrating and the Poles do the camping.
